title: Teaching Fish to Walk
subtitle: Beginner Bartending Development Course
overview: Teaching Fish to Walk is a foundational training programme designed to introduce new bartenders to the core skills, knowledge, and professional behaviours required in a modern bar environment. This one-day course provides a structured, academically grounded orientation to the responsibilities of bar work, legal compliance, practical skill-building, customer interaction, and product understanding.
objectives: Understand regulations and legal procedure for the service of alcohol.
Complete legally required certificates for food and beverage service.
Use bartending tools, techniques and machinery.
Achieve a competent level of customer service.
Develop knowledge in beer and wine.

topics.title: Alcohol Licensing
points: 2-hour alcohol licensing training.
Alcohol age awareness training.

topics.title: Food Safety and Hygiene
points: Food safety and hygiene training.
Allergen awareness training.

topics.title: Health and Safety
points: Health and safety training.

topics.title: Fire Safety
points: Fire safety training.
Participants granted recognised certifications upon completion.

topics.title: Using Bar Equipment
points: Properly and efficiently using jiggers and measures.
Understanding ice etiquette and how to cleanly handle ice.

topics.title: Serving Drinks
points: Understanding glass handling and safety.
Understanding presentation, consistency and recognising the importance.
Being proficient in wine service including opening bottles, table service and storage.
Pouring pints consistently and without waste.

topics.title: Bar Prep
points: Safely cutting and prepping garnishes.
Creating a list to effectively prep and stock the bar ready for service.

topics.title: Cleanliness and Organisation
points: Understanding the importance of a clean bar and how to maintain it.
Organising stations before service and maintaining them during.
Understanding efficient and smooth strategies of service with ways to adapt for personal styles.

topics.title: Using Machinery
points: Operating post mix guns and machines.
Operating and cleaning glass-washes.

topics.title: Cellar Use and Stock Management
points: Operating, maintaining and changing beer lines and kegs.
Understanding how to efficiently stock and maintain a cellar.

topics.title: Key Skills
points: Understanding valuable aspects of customer service and steps to deliver it.
Working naturally and in balance with guests.
Understanding the importance of welcoming guests and personalised service.
Remaining calm and composed within a high stress environment.

topics.title: Sales Techniques
points: Using suggestive selling, menu anchoring and the "Two Choice Close", and when it is appropriate to do so.
Using storytelling and product knowledge alongside situational selling to personalise guest interactions.
Understanding the importance of check-back timing and its effects.

topics.title: Dealing with Difficult Guests
points: Coping with frustration behind the bar.
Managing rude, aggressive and inappropriate guests and what action to take in specific situations.
Learning the correct way to say NO when refusing service or unable to complete an order.
Identifying the importance and use of team communication and back-up.
Understanding incident reporting procedures, ejection protocols and aftercare.

topics.title: Safety Working with Guests
points: Identifying inappropriate comments, touching or imitation and how to proceed.
Safely intervening if a guest is harassing another guest.
Recognising when to call security/management.
Handling sexual harassment and inappropriate behaviour towards yourself.
Understanding incident reporting procedures, ejection protocols and aftercare.

topics.title: Beer
points: A brief history of beer in Great Britain.
The production of beer.
The classification of beer, common regions, flavour profiles and popular brands.
Tasting.

topics.title: Wine
points: A brief history of wine throughout the world.
The production of wine.
The classification of wine, common regions, flavour profiles and popular styles.
Tasting.

assessment.notes: No assessment or test required. Attendance will grant a pass.
assessment.certification: Successful participants will receive a Certificate of Completion.

resources: Access to a functioning bar (no external equipment required).
Participants are encouraged to bring note taking stationery (optional).
policies: Students are expected to maintain professional behaviour, respect fellow learners, and adhere to health and safety standards.
Any participant that does not follow the Code of Conduct will be removed from the course and will not achieve the certification.
